title: B2. Sign-Sensitive Derivation (Exciton Rydberg Energy)
label: gemma426b_clean_benchmark_v1_2026_04_06
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B2-sign-sensitive-derivation-exciton-rydberg-energy
Title: B2. Sign-Sensitive Derivation (Exciton Rydberg Energy)

Question:
Band gap 3 eV, screened Coulomb, 1s exciton peak at 1 eV.
What is the Rydberg energy for n = 3?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.
The sign matters.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: -0.111 eV
JUSTIFICATION: The 1s binding energy is E_b(1s) = 1 eV. In a hydrogenic model, E_b(n) = E_b(1s) / n^2. For n=3, E_b(3) = 1/9 eV ≈ 0.111 eV. Since Rydberg energy refers to the energy level relative to the continuum (or the binding energy expressed as a negative value), the energy is -0.111 eV.
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: -0.111 eV
JUSTIFICATION: The 1s binding energy is E_b(1s) = 1 eV. In a hydrogenic model, E_b(n) = E_b(1s) / n^2. For n=3, E_b(3) = 1/9 eV ≈ 0.111 eV. Since Rydberg energy refers to the energy level relative to the continuum (or the binding energy expressed as a negative value), the energy is -0.111 eV.
